stimulus:
While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes:

  • Sasha Petrov is a Brooklyn-based ceramicist.
  • She uses the term “flora-ware” to describe the plant-inspired vessels that she sculpts.
  • “Flora-ware” is a combination of the words “flora” and “earthenware.”
  • Her piece Bloom is a teapot shaped like an open hibiscus flower.
  • Her piece Tendril is a vase formed from intertwined ivy stems.
stem: The student wants to provide an explanation and example of “flora-ware.” Which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal?

answerOptions.content: The term “flora-ware,” used by Brooklyn-based ceramicist Sasha Petrov, is a combination of the words “flora” and “earthenware.”
Sasha Petrov, who created Bloom, refers to her pieces as “flora-ware.”
Sasha Petrov uses the term “flora-ware,” a combination of the words “flora” and “earthenware,” to describe her plant-inspired vessels, such as a teapot shaped like an open hibiscus flower.
The pieces Bloom and Tendril are examples of “flora-ware,” a term that the ceramicist Sasha Petrov uses to describe her work.

rationale: Choice C is the best answer because it both explains "flora-ware" (a combination of flora and earthenware describing plant-inspired vessels) and gives a concrete example (a teapot shaped like a hibiscus). Choice A is incorrect because it explains the term but offers no example. Choice B is incorrect because it gives only a title without explaining what the term means. Choice D is incorrect because it cites two examples by name but does not explain what the term means.
